summary: French realist novelist, author of The Human Comedy (La Comédie Humaine), a vast cycle of interconnected novels depicting French society. Best known for Père Goriot, Cousin Bette, and The Unknown Masterpiece.

works.name: Père Goriot
works.description: Novel centered on the Maison Vauquer boardinghouse, featuring the old pasta maker Goriot and law student Eugène de Rastignac.

clues.clue: Set in Maison Vauquer boardinghouse; Goriot is an old pasta maker whose daughters Anastasie and Delphine do not attend his funeral
examples: centered on the Maison Vauquer boarders, including an old pasta maker whose daughters Anastasie and Delphine do not attend his funeral

clues.clue: Criminal Vautrin meets law student Eugène de Rastignac
examples: The criminal Vautrin meets the law student Eugene de Rastignac

clues.clue: Rastignac at end challenges Paris from Père Lachaise cemetery: "Now it's between us"
examples: Rastignac utters the line "Now it's between us" while looking at Paris from a cemetery

clues.clue: Vautrin is secretly the master criminal Jacques Collin / Trompe-la-Mort
examples: Vautrin is revealed to be the master criminal Jacques Collin

works.name: The Unknown Masterpiece
works.description: Short story about the painter Frenhofer and his obsessive painting.

clues.clue: A foot emerges from formless fog of a painting by Frenhofer; friends can only identify a "naked foot" amid chaos
examples: A foot emerges from the "dim, formless fog" of a painting by Frenhofer
two friends can only identify a "naked foot" amid a "formless fog"

clues.clue: Frenhofer dies in a fire overnight after the painting is revealed
examples: a man dies in a fire overnight after two friends can only identify a "naked foot"

works.name: Sarrasine
works.description: Story about a sculptor who falls in love with a castrato singer.

clues.clue: Protagonist cries out at seeing an "ideal beauty" in a Jomelli opera; Roland Barthes analyzed it in S/Z as "hermeneutic" and "proairetic"
examples: cries out when he sees an "ideal beauty" in a Jomelli opera, which a later author read as both "hermeneutic" and "proairetic"

works.name: Cousin Bette
works.description: Novel about the scheming poor relation Lisbeth Fischer.

clues.clue: Part of The Human Comedy; Lisbeth Fischer (Cousin Bette) schemes against the Hulot family
examples: this author included Pere Goriot and Cousin Bette in the cycle The Human Comedy

works.name: Other Works / General
works.description: The Human Comedy cycle, Ursule Mirouet, and general Balzac clues.

clues.clue: The Human Comedy (La Comédie Humaine) — vast interconnected cycle of novels about French society
examples: name this author of The Human Comedy

clues.clue: Titles a Dai Sijie novel: Balzac and the Little Chinese Seamstress — boys borrow books at reeducation camp
examples: This author partly titles a novel in which two boys borrow books from Four-Eyes at a reeducation camp, by Dai Sijie

clues.clue: Ursule Mirouet: Catholic doctor names pious girl as heir, will destroyed by postmaster
examples: a Catholic doctor names the title pious girl as an heir, but his will is destroyed by a postmaster

comprehensive_summary:
Honoré de Balzac (1799-1850) was a towering French realist novelist, author of The Human Comedy (La Comédie Humaine), a vast interconnected cycle of novels depicting French society across all social classes. His work is referenced in Dai Sijie's Balzac and the Little Chinese Seamstress, in which two boys borrow French literature at a reeducation camp.

Père Goriot is set in the Maison Vauquer boardinghouse in Paris. The title character is an old pasta maker whose two daughters, Anastasie and Delphine, drain his fortune and fail to attend his funeral. The criminal Vautrin (secretly the master criminal Jacques Collin / Trompe-la-Mort) meets the ambitious law student Eugène de Rastignac, who at the novel's end challenges Paris from Père Lachaise cemetery.

The Unknown Masterpiece is a story about the painter Frenhofer whose obsessive work produces only chaos from which a single foot emerges. Frenhofer dies in a fire. Sarrasine features a sculptor who falls for a castrato singer; Roland Barthes analyzed it in S/Z using "hermeneutic" and "proairetic" codes. Cousin Bette follows the scheming Lisbeth Fischer against the Hulot family. Ursule Mirouet involves a Catholic doctor whose will naming a pious girl as heir is destroyed by a postmaster.

cards.front: Novel: set in the Maison Vauquer boardinghouse
Novel: an old pasta maker's daughters Anastasie and Delphine drain his fortune and skip his funeral
Novel: the criminal Vautrin (Jacques Collin / Trompe-la-Mort) meets the law student Eugène de Rastignac at the Maison Vauquer
Short Story: a foot emerges from the "dim, formless fog" of the painter Frenhofer's obsessive masterpiece; he dies in a fire
Short Story: a sculptor falls for a castrato singer at a Jomelli opera
Short Story: analyzed by Barthes in S/Z as "hermeneutic" and "proairetic"
Author: titles a Dai Sijie novel about boys borrowing French literature at a reeducation camp
